intent: Add a `code_tutor` CLI that teaches you an unfamiliar codebase interactively: it analyses the code into items, builds a dependency-first learning plan, poses 'reimplement this' challenges, and verifies your implementation with tests and coverage, using a local LLM for hints.
scope: meta/pyproject.toml, meta/requirements.txt, .pre-commit-config.yaml (tooling config + deps: anthropic, rich, typer)
Non-goal: shipping a hosted service; the tutor runs locally against a local Ollama-compatible LLM.
changes: _analyzer.py: parse a codebase into CodeItem objects (functions/classes) via the ast module, with a content hash over file mtime/size and skip rules.
_plan_builder.py: order items dependency-first (topological sort of Python import edges via graphlib) and group them into learning sessions.
_challenge.py: pose reimplement-the-item challenges, discover matching user test functions via AST, and drive the attempt loop.
_verifier.py: run the user's implementation against tests and enforce a coverage bar.
_progress.py / _session.py: persist and track per-item progress and session state.
_llm.py: streaming client for a local Ollama-compatible LLM (line-delimited JSON, on_token callback).

meta/pyproject.toml: ruff/pylint max-args=6, max-returns=8, max-locals=20 for the challenge flows; mypy overrides for rich/typer/anthropic (no stubs) and for code_tutor's TYPE_CHECKING-only imports; allow AST NodeVisitor visit_CapWords method names.

verification.evidence: All code_tutor modules at 100% line and branch coverage

verification.command: pre-commit run (staged code_tutor package + tooling config)
verification.evidence: ruff (select=ALL), mypy strict, pylint 10/10, bandit, evidence + multi-file contract hooks all pass

risks: _llm.py talks to a local LLM endpoint; if none is running the hint feature is unavailable (challenges/verification still work offline).
The tutor executes user-supplied implementation code during verification; intended for local single-user use only.
rollback: git revert the commit; remove anthropic/rich/typer from requirements if nothing else uses them.
After rollback, run pre-commit run --all-files to confirm the lint/type config still validates without the code_tutor overrides.
